hints.studia: 'studia' is a noun in the nominative plural meaning 'studies'
hints.adulescentiam: 'adulescentiam' is a noun in the accusative meaning 'youth'

hints.to raise: 'to raise' is 'tollere' or 'erigere' — 'alunt' comes from 'alere' (to nourish)

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'alere' 3rd pl. would be 'alebant' — 'alunt' has the present -unt ending
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'alere' 3rd pl. would be 'aluerunt' — 'alunt' is the present form
hints.Future: Future of 'alere' 3rd pl. would be 'alent' — 'alunt' has the present -unt ending

hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the principal parts of 'agere' (to do/drive)
hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amare' (to love)
hints.audio, audire, audivi, auditum: These are the principal parts of 'audire' (to hear)

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'studium' (sg.) would be 'studii' — 'studia' is nominative or accusative neuter plural
hints.Accusative: While 'studia' could be accusative neuter plural, here it is the subject performing the action of nourishing
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural of 'studium' would be 'studiis' — 'studia' is nominative neuter plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'studii' ends in -ii
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'studii' ends in -ii
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'studii' ends in -ii

hints.adulescentiam: 'adulescentiam' is feminine accusative — 'Haec' here is neuter plural, agreeing with 'studia'
hints.alunt: 'alunt' is a verb — demonstrative pronouns modify nouns, not verbs
hints.Haec stands alone: 'Haec' agrees in gender and number with 'studia' (neuter plural) and modifies it

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'adulescentia' is 'adulescentia' — 'adulescentiam' has the -am accusative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'adulescentia' would be 'adulescentiae' — 'adulescentiam' ends in -am
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'adulescentia' would be 'adulescentia' — 'adulescentiam' has the -am accusative ending

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'adulescentiae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'adulescentiae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'adulescentiae' ends in -ae
